text:
The proposed work is titled as BEVDepth. It is a real-time 3D object detection framework which is developed to increase the reliability of the depth estimation for the camera-based Bird’s-Eye-View (BEV) perception. Depth is an essential element in order to project multi-view images onto 3D space. However, state-of-the-art “lift-splat-shoot” paradigm detectors generally provide highly inaccurate depth maps, and only a small portion of the correct features need to be localized for achieving accurate detection results.

Both BLUR and BLUR++ heavily rely on depth estimation from image cues. The dependency on image-based depth estimation leads to inaccurate depth information at obscure or distant points in the scene, as demonstrated in Figure 1. To alleviate this challenge, BEVDepth utilises explicit depth supervision based on point cloud data, along with a camera-aware depth estimation module that utilises the intrinsic and extrinsic camera parameters within the depth estimation process. In addition, the Depth Refinement Module (DRM) corrects feature unprojection errors. When paired with an improved voxel pooling kernel and multi-frame fusion, BEVDepth achieves state-of-the-art results of 60.9% NDS on the official nuScenes test set, becoming the first camera-only depth estimation approach to surpass the 60% NDS margin, while still maintaining high speeds.

prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
